item.title: Video shows massive drug-smuggling tunnel connecting U.S. and Mexico
item.description: Four people were arrested and charged with trafficking more than $45 million in cocaine through the 2,000-foot-long tunnel complete with reinforced walls, ventilation and a rail system.

item.title: Why Iowa's 2026 elections could have a national impact
item.description: If you want to understand the vast changes in American politics since 2008, changes that led to the Trump era, you must study Iowa, CBS News' Major Garrett argues. Amanda Rooker, chief political reporter for CBS Des Moines affiliate KCCI, joins to unpack the dynamics of Tuesday's primaries in the Hawkeye State.

item.title: Blanche says DOJ "not moving forward" with "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: "We are not moving forward with the fund. Period," Blanche told House lawmakers.

item.title: Senior Iranian officer says return to war with U.S. seems "inevitable"
item.description: Prospects for a U.S.-Iran deal seem to dim as the war between Israel and Hezbollah grinds on despite Trump saying they agreed to stop fighting.

item.title: Blanche tells Congress DOJ "not moving forward" with "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche told lawmakers Tuesday that the Justice Department will not move forward with a controversial "anti-weaponization" fund. Former Deputy Assistant Attorney General Tom Dupree joins with analysis.

item.title: Texas mom's killer received text before murder: "I hope you handle it"
item.description: After Alyssa Burkett was murdered, detectives quickly learned that the prime suspect was the father of her child, Andrew Beard. But as the investigation unfolded, they would find out that Beard wasn't the only one involved in the murder plot.

item.title: After woman's murder, detectives learn killer was "only half the story"
item.description: After Alyssa Burkett was murdered in broad daylight in Carrollton, Texas, Andrew Beard, the father of her child, became a suspect. Investigators would eventually discover a twisted murder plot they say was orchestrated by his fiancée, Holly Elkins.

item.title: Frontier flight to Chicago diverted to Miami when passenger tries to open door mid-flight
item.description: A Frontier Airlines flight from Puerto Rico to Chicago O'Hare International Airport Sunday was diverted to Miami when a passenger tried to open the door mid-flight.

item.title: Some companies question cost of using AI over human workers
item.description: Developments in artificial intelligence are happening very quickly, and so are mass layoffs as corporations rapidly embrace the technology. But now some companies like Uber and Microsoft are starting to second-guess aggressive AI adoption. Reyhan Harmanci, director of features at Wired magazine, joins to discuss the state of AI in corporate America.

item.title: Justice Department charges 4 people in connection with drug-smuggling tunnel connecting U.S., Mexico
item.description: Federal authorities have arrested and charged four people with drug trafficking after a months-long investigation uncovered what federal officials called a massive, 2,000-foot-long underground tunnel connecting Mexico to the U.S. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has the details.

item.title: Gas prices likely to remain high for months, energy experts say
item.description: "It will be a very long, multi-month to multi-year process for things to fully normalize," GasBuddy's Patrick De Haan said.

item.title: Watch: Acting AG Blanche and Rep. Dean have heated exchange over Epstein investigation
item.description: Rep. Madeleine Dean had a tense line of questioning for acting Attorney General Todd Blanche Tuesday over the state of the Jeffrey Epstein investigation. See the moment from the House Appropriations Committee hearing.

item.title: GOP Rep. Kean returning to in-person work in "matter of weeks" after absence
item.description: The New Jersey congressman last voted on March 5.

item.title: Video: Russia unleashes massive barrage of drones, missiles across Ukraine
item.description: Russia launched a barrage of drones and missiles across Ukraine overnight, killing at least 13 people and injuring dozens more. These videos show the attacks and their aftermath.

item.title: Pilot of fighter jet downed over Iran previously shot down in Kuwaiti incident
item.description: About a month after ejecting during the friendly-fire incident, the pilot was on a mission over Iran when his jet was hit by a surface-to-air missile, prompting a daring rescue operation.

item.title: Sneak peek: The Plot to Eliminate Alyssa Burkett
item.description: ENCORE: A toxic couple orchestrates an elaborate plan to kill a mother. "48 Hours" correspondent Peter Van Sant reports Saturday, June 6 at 10/9c* on CBS and streaming on Paramount+. (Following the WNBA on CBS)

item.title: Amazon faces lawsuit over Ring facial recognition software
item.description: A Virginia man is suing Amazon over Ring's "Familiar Faces" feature, alleging the technology violates people's privacy.

item.title: The main reason some young college grads are struggling to find work
item.description: Economists at the New York Federal Reserve say they've identified the main reason some recent college grads are having trouble landing a job.

item.title: Flesh-eating screwworm detected 25 miles from U.S. border, USDA says
item.description: A flesh-eating New World screwworm was recently detected in Mexico just 25 miles from the United States border, according to the USDA.

item.title: What travelers should know about Europe's new Entry/Exit System
item.description: Travel experts say to be prepared for potential disruptions as countries implement the new Entry/Exit System now in place across the EU and other countries.

item.title: Blue Origin vows to resume New Glenn launches despite explosion
item.description: Damage to Blue Origin's lone launch pad in the wake of last week's spectacular explosion was not as severe as initially feared, the company said.

item.title: Trump names controversial housing official Bill Pulte as acting intel chief
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday announced he's tapping housing official Bill Pulte to serve as the acting director of national intelligence to replace Tulsi Gabbard.

item.title: Russian missile, drone attack on Ukraine kills at least 22, officials say
item.description: Russian missile, drone strikes kill at least 22 people across Ukraine, authorities say, after President Zelenskyy warned Moscow was planning a "massive new strike."

item.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner rescheduled for July 24
item.description: Prosecutors have accused Cole Allen of attempting to assassinate the president when he allegedly attacked the White House Correspondents' Dinner on April 25.

item.title: Legendary wine unveiled after decades hidden under castle floor
item.description: The collection once belonged to the noble Beaufort-Spontin family, who were suspected of having collaborated with the Nazis.

item.title: Vanilla Ice says he'd perform for anybody, including Putin and Iran
item.description: "It's not anything to do with politics. I don't know why they're turning it into politics," Vanilla Ice said of the Freedom 250 concerts planned in Washington, D.C.

item.title: NASA taps 2 companies to develop buggies for its moon base program
item.description: The FLEX Rover will be equipped to carry two astronauts and traverse hundreds of miles of lunar terrain.

item.title: Ex-boyfriend and fiancée behind fatal stabbing, shooting of Texas mom
item.description: What appeared to be an open-and-shut case for Texas investigators turned out to be a twisted murder plot involving victim Alyssa Beard's ex-boyfriend Andrew Beard and his fiancée Holly Elkins – who detectives say was the mastermind.

item.title: Aurora Ethiopian leader set on writing his community's story
item.description: Samuel GebreMichael leads the Colorado Ethiopian Community and also serves on the Aurora Immigrant and Refugee Commission.

item.title: Korean immigrant champions cause of Aurora's global residents
item.description: Song is especially proud to manage Aurora's language access plan, where she helps all newcomers access city services in their native tongue.
